skill.name: nano-banana
skill.description: Generate images with Google Gemini native image models via inference.sh CLI. Models: Gemini 3 Pro Image, Gemini 2.5 Flash Image. Capabilities: text-to-image, image editing, multi-image input. Triggers: nano banana, gemini image, gemini 3 pro image, gemini 2.5 flash image, google image generation, native image generation, gemini native image
skill.summary: Generate images using Google Gemini 3 Pro and Gemini 2.5 Flash models through inference.sh CLI
skill.author: toolshell

security_audit.summary: Static analysis detected 40 potential issues, all are false positives. The 'external_commands' patterns are Markdown code blocks containing bash examples, not executable code. The 'network' findings are legitimate documentation links to inference.sh platform. The 'weak cryptography' flags incorrectly identified model version numbers (3-pro, 2-5-flash) as cryptographic algorithm references. This skill is documentation-only with no executable code, no prompt injection attempts, and legitimate intent to help users generate images via Google Gemini models.

content.user_title: Generate Images with Google Gemini Models
content.value_statement: Generate images using Google Gemini 3 Pro and Gemini 2.5 Flash through Claude Code. This skill provides direct access to Google's native image generation models with support for text-to-image, image editing, and multi-image input workflows.
actual_capabilities: Generate images from text prompts using Google Gemini 3 Pro Image model
Generate images quickly using Google Gemini 2.5 Flash Image model
Edit existing images with text prompts and image input
Generate multiple images in a single request
Enable Google Search grounding for real-time context in image generation
limitations: Requires inference.sh CLI installation and authentication
Requires Bash tool permission with infsh command access
Depends on external inference.sh service availability
Image generation speed varies by model (Flash is faster, Pro is higher quality)

use_cases.title: Generate Marketing Visuals
use_cases.description: Create product images, social media graphics, and marketing materials with custom prompts and aspect ratios

use_cases.title: Edit and Enhance Images
use_cases.description: Modify existing images by adding elements, changing styles, or enhancing specific features with text prompts

use_cases.title: Prototype Visual Concepts
use_cases.description: Quickly generate multiple visual concepts and variations for brainstorming and concept validation

prompt_templates.title: Generate a Basic Image
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate an image of [describe your subject] in [style, e.g., photorealistic style] with [specific details about composition, lighting, mood]
prompt_templates.scenario: Use this template when you need to create a new image from scratch with specific visual requirements

prompt_templates.title: Edit an Existing Image
prompt_templates.prompt: Edit the image at [image URL or path] to [describe the change you want, e.g., add a sunset in the background, change the color scheme to blue tones]
prompt_templates.scenario: Use this template when you have an existing image that needs modifications or enhancements

prompt_templates.title: Generate Multiple Variations
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate 4 different variations of [subject description] with [style] showing different [angles/perspectives/moods]
prompt_templates.scenario: Use this template when you want to explore multiple creative directions for the same concept

prompt_templates.title: Create High-Resolution Output
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a 4K resolution image of [detailed subject description] with [specific lighting, textures, and atmospheric details] in aspect ratio 16:9
prompt_templates.scenario: Use this template when you need high-quality output for print or large-format displays

output_examples.input: Generate a photorealistic image of a banana floating in space with Earth visible in the background, golden hour lighting
output_examples.output: Generated image URL from inference.sh showing a detailed banana in space with Earth, dramatic lighting, and realistic textures

output_examples.input: Create 4 minimalist logo designs for a coffee shop, black and white, geometric style
output_examples.output: Array of 4 image URLs with different minimalist coffee logo concepts using geometric shapes

output_examples.input: Edit this image to add a rainbow in the sky: https://example.com/landscape.jpg
output_examples.output: Generated image URL showing the original landscape with a vibrant rainbow added across the sky

best_practices: Be specific in your prompts about style, lighting, composition, and mood for better results
Use aspect ratio parameter to match your output medium (16:9 for video, 1:1 for social media, 9:16 for mobile)
Start with Gemini 2.5 Flash for faster iterations, then use Gemini 3 Pro for final high-quality renders
anti_patterns: Do not generate images that violate copyright laws or replicate trademarked characters/logos
Avoid vague prompts like generate a nice image without specific details about subject, style, or composition
Do not use this skill for generating inappropriate, offensive, or harmful content

faq.question: What is the difference between Gemini 3 Pro and Gemini 2.5 Flash?
faq.answer: Gemini 3 Pro Image produces the highest quality images but is slower. Gemini 2.5 Flash Image generates images quickly with excellent quality. Use Flash for iterations and Pro for final outputs.

faq.question: Do I need to install anything before using this skill?
faq.answer: Yes, you need the inference.sh CLI installed. Run npx skills add inference-sh/skills@agent-tools to install it, then authenticate with infsh login.

faq.question: Can I edit existing images with this skill?
faq.answer: Yes, you can edit images by providing the image URL in the images parameter along with a prompt describing the changes you want to make.

faq.question: What aspect ratios are supported?
faq.answer: You can choose from 1:1 (square), 16:9 (landscape), 9:16 (portrait), 4:3 (standard landscape), 3:4 (standard portrait), or auto for automatic selection.

faq.question: What is Google Search grounding?
faq.answer: Google Search grounding enables the model to use real-time information from Google Search to inform image generation, useful for current events or specific factual details.

faq.question: How many images can I generate at once?
faq.answer: You can generate multiple images in a single request using the num_images parameter. For editing, you can provide up to 14 input images.
